findings.location: s1 — MessageSenderを使った送受信処理 (SendProjectInsertMessageAction.java)
findings.description: The Java code example in s1 is significantly truncated. The source shows a complete try-catch block where MessagingException is caught and TransactionAbnormalEnd(100, e, "error.sendServer.fail") is thrown as the error handling pattern. The source also shows `responseMessage.getDataRecord()` as the method for retrieving response data after a successful send. Both patterns are omitted from the knowledge file's code example.
findings.source_evidence: catch (MessagingException e) { throw new TransactionAbnormalEnd(100, e, "error.sendServer.fail"); } ... Map<String, Object> responseDataRecord = responseMessage.getDataRecord();

findings.location: s2 — 電文を登録する際に使用するフォームクラス (ProjectInsertMessageForm.java)
findings.description: The form class implementation example is omitted. The source shows the pattern for accessing record data from RequestMessage using `message.getRecordOf("userData")` to obtain a DataRecord, then calling `data.getString("projectName")` etc. This demonstrates the concrete API usage pattern for reading received message fields.
The hint `TransactionAbnormalEnd` is missing. The source code example uses this class as the recommended error handling class when MessagingException is caught during sync message send.

Missing hint `LpadFormatter` (nablarch.common.idgenerator.formatter.LpadFormatter), which is used as the concrete class for `receivedSequenceFormatter` in the AsyncMessageReceiveActionSettings configuration. Also missing property name hints: `receivedSequenceGenerator`, `targetGenerateId`, `sqlFilePackage` which appear in the configuration XML.
findings.source_evidence: <component class="nablarch.common.idgenerator.formatter.LpadFormatter"> ... <property name="receivedSequenceGenerator" ref="idGenerator" /> <property name="targetGenerateId" value="9991" /> <property name="sqlFilePackage" value="com.nablarch.example.sql" />
